cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out between what times Andrew could have gone to the clothing store. First, I need to look at all the times when Andrew was seen doing other activities and see when he was free. The clothing store was closed after 9pm, so the latest he could have gone is before 9pm. Andrew woke up at 5am. Then, William saw him playing tennis from 5am to 6am. So from 5 to 6am, he was at the tennis court. Then Jessica saw him buying lunch at the deli from 6am to 9am. So from 6 to 9am, he's at the deli. Tiffany saw Andrew taking photos near the Leaning Tower of Pisa from 9am to 11am. That means he's busy there from 9 to 11am. John saw Andrew waiting at the train station from 11am to 5pm. So from 11am until 5pm, he's at the train station. Sarah saw Andrew reading at the library from 6pm to 9pm. So he's at the library from 6 to 9pm. Now, the clothing store was closed after 9pm, so the latest possible time he could have gone is before 9pm. We need to find the time when Andrew was not doing any of the above activities. Let's list his schedule: - 5am - 6am: Tennis - 6am - 9am: Deli - 9am - 11am: Pisa photos - 11am - 5pm: Train station - 6pm - 9pm: Library Looking at the timeline, the only time gaps are: From 5am to 6am (tennis), then 6am to 9am (deli), then 9am to 11am (photos), then 11am to 5pm (train station), and then 5pm to 6pm (no activity mentioned), then 6pm to 9pm (library). So the only time when Andrew is unaccounted for is between 5pm and 6pm. Because after 5pm, from 5pm to 6pm, there's no activity listed. Then he's at the library from 6pm to 9pm.
